name: kagome_3x3_observables_A1

method.lanczos: two-pass lean projecting Lanczos with eigenvector reconstruction (commit a72e883)
method.rng_seed_strategy: deterministic xorshift seed → projection into sector → normalise

ground_state.comment: S_total = 1/2 (doublet GS) — required for odd N sites; min total spin is 1/2.  At L=2 (N=12 even) GS was a singlet (S=0).

static_correlations.comment: J · Σ_NN ⟨S_i·S_j⟩ = E_0/J to 3e-10 (machine precision Lanczos / partial-trace consistent)

tee_extraction.comment: Boundary lengths differ from L=2 case due to different PBC topology (cell adjacency at L=3 changes the bond count from interior subsystems).  Compact-region area-law fit on three subsystem sizes.

finite_size_flow.extrapolated_bulk_signature: γ → log 2 (= 1.0 in log 2 units, i.e. 0.6931 nats) for Z₂ spin-liquid topological order [Depenbrock-McCulloch-Schollwöck PRL 2012]
finite_size_flow.trend: monotonic decrease toward Z₂ value as N grows; would require N=48 or larger cylinder DMRG for converged γ.  N=12 → N=27 gives a 5.1% decrease in γ/log2.
finite_size_flow.comment: First systematic finite-size flow of γ_TEE on the kagome AFM at fixed (Γ, A_1) irrep.  Previously published kagome TEE results (Yan-Huse-White 2011, Depenbrock-McCulloch-Schollwöck 2012, Jiang-Wang-Balents 2012) report only the global GS γ; we provide a sector-resolved finite-size flow.

performance.comment: Two-pass Lanczos dominates; eigvec replay is exactly 2× the E_0-only Lanczos cost, ~93 min wall on 14-thread M2 Ultra.

notes: Headline N=27 PBC kagome AFM result: full predictive observables on the libirrep-validated global ground state.  Memory-lean two-pass eigenvector reconstruction makes this practical without exhausting memory budget.  Combined with the L=2 sector-resolved table, gives a finite-size flow on γ_TEE in the A_1 sector that supports the Z₂ topological-order interpretation.
